Arturo, and contributions from Precious Metal assets which were acquired or commenced production over the past year. Revenue
Precious Metal assets accounted for 90% of our revenue in Q4 2025 (71% gold, 17% silver, and 2% PGM). Revenue was sourced
• Acquisition of Royalty Portfolio from Victoria Gold Corp. – Canada and U.S.:
Subsequent to year-end, on February 24, 2026, we agreed to acquire a portfolio of six royalties previously held by Victoria Gold Corp.
in the Yukon. The portfolio also includes a milestone payment royalty on i-80 Gold Corp.’s Cove project in Nevada and three additional
royalties on earlier stage properties in Nevada and the Yukon. Closing of the transaction is expected to occur in H1 2026.
• Acquisition of Royalty on Bullabulling Gold Project with Minerals 260 – Australia:
(approximately $120 million) gross royalty from Minerals 260 Limited to support its development of the Bullabulling gold project
located in Western Australia. The royalty consists of a 1.45% gross royalty over certain tenements on which Franco-Nevada already
held a 1.00% royalty and a new 2.45% gross royalty over tenements where Franco-Nevada did not already hold an existing royalty.
Upon production of an aggregate 4.0 Moz Au from royalty lands, the royalties, in aggregate, will step down from 2.45% to 1.63%.
will be funded upon obtaining approval from the Foreign Investment Review Board. Additionally, Franco-Nevada subscribed for
A$50 million ($35.5 million) of Minerals 260’s ordinary shares at a price of A$0.45 per share.
• Acquisition of Royalty with i-80 Gold Corp – Nevada, U.S.:
NSR from i-80 Gold. The royalty consists of a 1.5% NSR increasing to 3.0% in 2031 on all minerals produced from Granite Creek,
the Ruby Hill Complex (including Archimedes and Mineral Point), Cove and Lone Tree. Funding of the upfront payment of $225 million
will be made upon closing, with a further $25 million payment subject to completion of 2026 budgeted spending at Mineral Point.
• Acquisition of Royalty on Yilgarn Star Gold Mine – Australia:
are not included in our 2026 guidance. Timing of the receipt of such deliveries depends on the timing of formal approval by the GOP .
Guidance and Outlook
Our 2026 guidance and five-year outlook are based on assumptions including the forecasted state of operations from our assets
Our 2026 guidance and five-year outlook are based on the following assumed commodity prices: $4,500/oz Au, $75.00/oz Ag,
2026 Guidance
Beginning in 2026, we will be adopting fixed GEO conversion ratios based on the pricing assumptions outlined in our 2026 guidance.
